content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
NEUTRAL — normal volatility 35.9% with extremely narrow participation (breadth 0/8); market direction is unclear and risk is concentrated.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Holding & Investment is the relative leader with DOHOL (1W: +0.60% | 1M: +12.10%) ABOVE TREND; Heavy Industry is the next-best grouping but mixed: ASELS (1W: -1.32% | 1M: +0.49%) CORRECTION and EREGL (1W: -2.67% | 1M: +16.16%) CORRECTION.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Energy & Chemicals is the weakest: TUPRS (1W: -4.10% | 1M: -4.67%) BELOW TREND and SASA (1W: -11.96% | 1M: -13.96%) BELOW TREND; Technology & Telecom and Banking are also underperforming with KONTR (1W: -14.89% | 1M: -44.56%) BELOW TREND and AKBNK (1W: -12.09% | 1M: -22.30%) BELOW TREND.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
DOHOL (1W: +0.60% | 1M: +12.10%) ABOVE TREND; ASELS (1W: -1.32% | 1M: +0.49%) CORRECTION; ALARK (1W: -1.33% | 1M: +2.01%) CORRECTION; TOASO (1W: -2.09% | 1M: +4.11%) CORRECTION; EREGL (1W: -2.67% | 1M: +16.16%) CORRECTION.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
VESTL (1W: -10.34% | 1M: -11.84%) BELOW TREND; GUBRF (1W: -10.44% | 1M: +3.52%) CORRECTION; TRMET (1W: -11.30% | 1M: -21.45%) BELOW TREND; PETKM (1W: -11.37% | 1M: +3.29%) CORRECTION; SASA (1W: -11.96% | 1M: -13.96%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
High-momentum losers dominating: HEKTS (1W: -17.77% | 1M: +10.34%) CORRECTION; KONTR (1W: -14.89% | 1M: -44.56%) BELOW TREND; YKBNK (1W: -13.11% | 1M: -15.13%) BELOW TREND; TKFEN (1W: -12.48% | 1M: -1.94%) BELOW TREND; AKBNK (1W: -12.09% | 1M: -22.30%) BELOW TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking is under pressure and tactically underweight: AKBNK (1W: -12.09% | 1M: -22.30%) BELOW TREND; GARAN (1W: -8.53% | 1M: -15.07%) BELOW TREND; ISCTR (1W: -7.17% | 1M: -11.88%) BELOW TREND — watch earnings and NPL guidance for any relief.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy Industry is mixed with valuation-led divergence: EREGL (1W: -2.67% | 1M: +16.16%) CORRECTION and KRDMD (1W: -4.49% | 1M: +11.39%) CORRECTION versus VESTL (1W: -10.34% | 1M: -11.84%) BELOW TREND and FROTO (1W: -7.06% | 1M: -19.21%) BELOW TREND — favor selective exposure to earnings-backed names.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy & Chemicals are weakest cyclically: TUPRS (1W: -4.10% | 1M: -4.67%) BELOW TREND; PETKM (1W: -11.37% | 1M: +3.29%) CORRECTION; SASA (1W: -11.96% | 1M: -13.96%) BELOW TREND — commodity and FX moves are primary near-term drivers.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Adopt a defensive, selective stance: trim broad cyclicals and large bank exposure, hold cash/light hedges and reallocate to idiosyncratic winners (DOHOL, EREGL) and short-term recovery candidates while monitoring macro triggers; avoid averaging into broad market positions until breadth improves.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
DOHOL (1W: +0.60% | 1M: +12.10%) ABOVE TREND for momentum continuation; AKBNK (1W: -12.09% | 1M: -22.30%) BELOW TREND for risk/reward on any policy/earnings catalyst; EREGL (1W: -2.67% | 1M: +16.16%) CORRECTION for cyclical re-rating; KONTR (1W: -14.89% | 1M: -44.56%) BELOW TREND as a watch for technical relief.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Banking & Finance for policy and earnings flow; Energy & Chemicals for commodity/FX sensitivity; Heavy Industry for any earnings-led divergences.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 reaction to the 20-day moving average (near-term support) and the monthly high (near-term resistance); key catalysts are CPI and FX moves, CBRT comments, and major bank/industrial quarterly updates — momentum break below the 20-DMA would increase downside risk, reclaim above monthly highs would signal stabilization.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Market is NEUTRAL with normal volatility (35.9%) but zero sector breadth; downside is concentrated in banks, tech and energy. Defensive, selective positioning is advised: favor idiosyncratic longs (DOHOL, EREGL) and avoid broad exposure until breadth and technicals improve; monitor macro catalysts (inflation, FX, bank earnings) for directional signals.
